prestazioni: Engine swap to a modernized Rolls-Royce B60 6.0L inline-6 (originally used in post-war models like the Silver Wraith) with forged internals, including a billet crankshaft from Bryant Racing, Carrillo H-beam connecting rods, and JE forged pistons (10.5:1 compression ratio) for high-octane fuel compatibility. Pair with a custom-built centrifugal supercharger system by Vortech (V-23 trim) running 12 psi of boost, intercooled via a custom air-to-water setup with a PWR radiator and Bosch 044 fuel pump to support 500+ hp on 110-octane aviation fuel.

Full drivetrain reinforcement: Replace the original 4-speed manual transmission with a Tremec TKX 5-speed (close-ratio) paired to a Tilton twin-disc ceramic clutch. Install a custom 8.5-inch limited-slip differential from Quaife (ATB helical LSD) with 3.73:1 gearing, and upgrade the driveshaft to a carbon-fiber unit from The Driveshaft Shop to handle increased torque.

Suspension overhaul with a custom-fabricated double-wishbone front and multi-link rear setup, utilizing Öhlins TTX40 coilovers (3-way adjustable) with custom valving for the Wraith’s 3,500 lb weight. Pair with polyurethane bushings from SuperPro (95A durometer) and a custom-fabricated front sway bar (25mm solid) from Hotchkis. Braking system features 14-inch two-piece rotors from AP Racing (front) and 13-inch rotors (rear) with 6-piston calipers (front) and 4-piston calipers (rear), using Ferodo DS2500 pads for vintage aesthetics with modern stopping power.
estetica: Custom hand-formed aluminum body panels by RML Group, replicating the original Wraith’s lines but with subtle flared fenders (1.5-inch wider front, 2-inch wider rear) to accommodate modern 18x9.5 (front) and 18x11 (rear) wire-spoke wheels from Borrani (3-piece construction) wrapped in Pirelli P Zero Corsa tires (245/40R18 front, 295/35R18 rear). The wheels feature a polished aluminum rim with a brushed nickel center cap and stainless steel spokes.

Aerodynamic enhancements include a custom front splitter and rear diffuser fabricated from carbon fiber by C-West, designed to mimic the original Wraith’s grille shape while generating 150 lbs of downforce at 100 mph. The hood features a functional heat extractor vent (louvered design) to expel supercharger heat, while the rear fenders incorporate discreet side skirts with integrated LED turn signals (Luxxvision).

Interior reimagined with a mix of vintage and modern materials: Recaro Pole Position seats (retrimmed in Bridge of Weir leather with Alcantara inserts) mounted on custom aluminum sliders, a MOMO Prototipo steering wheel (380mm diameter) with a quick-release hub, and a bespoke dashboard featuring Smiths gauges (3.5-inch speedometer, 4-inch tachometer) alongside a Motec C127 digital display for boost, fuel pressure, and oil temperature. The rear compartment features a custom aluminum roll cage (TIG-welded) with leather-wrapped crossbars, and the original wood trim is replaced with carbon fiber veneer from Fiber Dynamics.
